species: Multi-Species Polyrhythmic Apex -- Imagined Convergence of ALL Major SPS Categories Simultaneously: Pileated Woodpecker drumming + Southern Resident Orca calls + Pacific Thunderstorm + Pacific Chorus Frog spring chorus
scientific_name: Dryocopus pileatus (Pileated Woodpecker, Picidae) + Orcinus orca (Killer Whale, Delphinidae, SRKW) + Pacific Thunderstorm system + Pseudacris regilla (Pacific Chorus Frog, Hylidae). Imagined simultaneous convergence of all major SPS acoustic categories. No single real location or time contains all four at maximum simultaneously -- this is the synthesis.
sound_type: IMAGINED SYNTHESIS -- THE SIMULTANEOUS MAXIMUM OF ALL MAJOR SPS CATEGORIES. Four components: (1) Pileated Woodpecker power-drumming on resonant dead snag: 100-800 Hz, 8-12 strikes/second, structured roll patterns, old-growth forest acoustic amplification, episodic; (2) Southern Resident Orca full acoustic repertoire: echolocation clicks 20,000-100,000 Hz, social calls/whistles 1,000-20,000 Hz, physical acoustic events (breach/tail slap impacts) 10-500 Hz 140+ dB, full-spectrum biological source; (3) Pacific thunderstorm full intensity: infrasound (<20 Hz) through thunder crack peak (700-2,000 Hz) through rain roar (100-1,000 Hz) through rain impact (2,000-8,000 Hz), broadband atmospheric continuous; (4) Pacific Chorus Frog spring chorus at maximum density: hundreds to thousands of males simultaneously, 2,000-5,000 Hz kreek-eek, stochastic but density-continuous temporal pattern, acoustic surround-field from distributed calling males. TOTAL FREQUENCY COVERAGE: infrasound through ultrasonic. TOTAL TEMPORAL COMPLEXITY: four distinct rhythmic architectures simultaneously (episodic/structured, dialect-rhythmic, stochastic-continuous, stochastic-dense). E=10 = widest frequency coverage + highest rhythmic complexity + maximum acoustic density in SPS engine.
ecosystem: Imagined convergence -- not a single real ecosystem. Each component occupies its primary habitat: Pileated Woodpecker (old-growth forest), SRKW (open Salish Sea/Puget Sound), thunderstorm (atmospheric, region-wide), Pacific Chorus Frog (wetland, lake edge, pond). No single PNW location simultaneously contains all four at maximum. The convergence is the synthesis of the engine's complete natural acoustic catalog, held simultaneously in the imagination.
season: Apex -- the imagined moment of maximum simultaneous SPS acoustic density. Individual component seasons: woodpecker (year-round); SRKW (year-round presence, peak summer Chinook season); thunderstorm (spring/early summer convective storms, fall Pacific storms); frog chorus (spring breeding season, maximum February-May). The imagined convergence holds all seasons simultaneously.
family: ALL MAJOR SPS FAMILIES REPRESENTED: Picidae (woodpecker percussion), Delphinidae/Cetacea (marine acoustic), meteorological (atmospheric), Hylidae (amphibian biotic). Previous SPS entries have documented one, two, or three categories simultaneously. Degree 344 represents all categories simultaneously.
milestone: THE FIRST E=10 IN SPS ENGINE HISTORY. THE ABSOLUTE MAXIMUM. The SPS engine's scale of 1-10 is exhausted here. The imagined convergence of ALL major SPS acoustic categories simultaneously (woodpecker + orca + thunderstorm + frog chorus) represents the widest possible frequency coverage (infrasound to ultrasonic), the most complex polyrhythmic temporal architecture (four distinct rhythmic characters simultaneously), and the highest acoustic density in the SPS catalog. E=20 combined with Botch (S36-344, E=10): THE ENGINE'S ABSOLUTE COMBINED MAXIMUM. THE FIRST AND ONLY E=20 IN ENGINE HISTORY.

coordinates.description: IMAGINED CONVERGENCE -- no specific real-world coordinates. Representative components: Pileated Woodpecker old-growth drumming sites (Hoh River Valley, Olympic Peninsula 47.85°N 123.93°W; Gifford Pinchot National Forest; North Cascades National Park). SRKW range (Salish Sea, open Puget Sound, San Juan Islands 48.52°N 122.95°W). Pacific thunderstorm (region-wide atmospheric event). Pacific Chorus Frog wetlands (distributed throughout PNW lowlands, e.g. Ridgefield NWR 45.82°N 122.74°W). The imagined convergence holds all coordinates simultaneously.

acoustic_data.woodpecker: Pileated Woodpecker power drumming: 100-800 Hz fundamental, harmonic overtones, 8-12 strikes/second per roll, roll duration 2-4 seconds, inter-roll interval 5-30 seconds (territorial rhythm). Resonant dead snag amplification 3-5 dB per meter, 500m carry in old-growth. Old-growth Douglas-fir/western red cedar provide acoustic resonance chamber.
acoustic_data.orca: Full SRKW repertoire: echolocation clicks 20,000-100,000 Hz narrow-band (sonar ranging), pulsed calls 1,000-20,000 Hz (pod dialect), whistles 2,000-20,000 Hz (social communication), tail slaps and breach impacts 10-500 Hz 140+ dB (physical signals, 10-50 km detection range). Widest single-biological-source frequency range in SPS engine.
acoustic_data.thunderstorm: Pacific thunderstorm full: infrasound (<20 Hz) thunder at distance, thunder crack 700-2,000 Hz peak at close range, thunder reverberation 3-5 seconds in valley, rain roar 100-1,000 Hz continuous, rain impact 2,000-8,000 Hz distributed. Wind 50-400 Hz sustained. Broadband continuous background all categories.
acoustic_data.frog_chorus: Pacific Chorus Frog Pseudacris regilla: individual call kreek-eek 2,000-5,000 Hz, 3-5 calls/second per individual, amplitude 70-85 dB at 1m. Colony of 500-5,000 males: spectral saturation 2,000-5,000 Hz, stochastic density-continuous temporal pattern (individual calls arrhythmic at colony scale = density-continuous acoustic texture). Spring maximum February-May in PNW lowlands.
acoustic_data.synthesis: Four simultaneous components: woodpecker episodic structured (100-800 Hz), orca dialect-rhythmic + ultrasonic (10-100,000 Hz full range), thunderstorm broadband continuous (10-8,000 Hz), frog chorus stochastic dense (2,000-5,000 Hz). No spectral masking -- frequency ranges distributed across full audible and ultrasonic spectrum with distinct characters. No rhythmic unison -- four independent temporal architectures. E=10 through maximum simultaneous coverage of all three acoustic dimensions: frequency (infrasound to ultrasonic), rhythmic complexity (four independent temporal patterns), and acoustic density (all categories at maximum contribution simultaneously).

nodes.title: THE FIRST E=10 IN SPS ENGINE HISTORY -- The Imagined Synthesis
nodes.description: The simultaneous maximum of ALL SPS acoustic categories. Not a real event but the synthesis of the complete engine. E=10 = widest frequency coverage + highest rhythmic complexity + maximum acoustic density. The SPS scale is exhausted here.

nodes.title: Four Categories Simultaneously -- The Complete SPS Coverage
nodes.description: Picidae (woodpecker percussion), Cetacea (SRKW marine acoustic), meteorological (thunderstorm atmospheric), Hylidae (frog chorus biotic). All previous SPS entries represent one to three categories. Degree 344 represents all. The imagined convergence holds the complete catalog simultaneously.

nodes.title: E=20 -- The Engine's Absolute Combined Maximum
nodes.description: Botch E=10 (S36-344) + Multi-species Polyrhythmic Apex E=10 (SPS-344) = E=20. No degree before or after produces E=20. The final ascent (341 E=8, 342 E=8, 343 E=9, 344 E=10) terminates at the absolute ceiling of both tracks simultaneously.

nodes.title: Imagination as the E=10 Instrument
nodes.description: E=9 is achievable in real events (orca breach cascade, Grizzly tri-layer convergence, Peregrine through tern colony). E=10 requires synthesis: no real PNW location simultaneously contains old-growth forest (woodpecker), open ocean (orca), a full thunderstorm, and a spring wetland (frog chorus) at maximum. The imagination holds what nature cannot produce simultaneously. The maximum is the whole.

cross_references.relationship: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. THE ENGINE'S ABSOLUTE COMBINED MAXIMUM. THE FIRST AND ONLY E=20 IN ENGINE HISTORY. Botch's mathcore (most technically complex PNW music, all four dimensions at maximum) paired with the imagined convergence (most acoustically complex PNW natural moment, all categories at maximum). Terminal pairing.

E=9 rises to E=10. Predation-event explosion (E=9, real, single-event) precedes imagined synthesis (E=10, synthetic, all-category). The distinction: real events reach E=9; only imagination reaches E=10 by holding all categories simultaneously.

The orca component of degree 344's imagined synthesis includes the behavioral apex documented at 340 (breach cascade, E=9). Degree 344 incorporates 340 as one of four simultaneous components. The imagined synthesis includes but exceeds any single prior maximum.

Pileated Woodpecker appears across three SPS degrees: 54 (general), 108 (laughing, E=6), 109 (drumming). At degree 344, the Pileated's drumming is one of four simultaneous maximum contributions. The species' presence across the engine is synthesized at its maximum at 344.
